desc: Rovinj is Istria at its prettiest: a pastel old town rising into St Euphemia, pine-edged swim coves, Venetian harbour light and a polished food scene that still feels rooted in place. It works brilliantly for romantic coastal breaks, slow evenings and people who want Adriatic atmosphere without the scale and churn of Croatia's biggest-name cities.

todo.name: St Euphemia Church
todo.desc: Climb through the old town to the church for the best rooftops-and-sea perspective.

todo.name: Old town harbour loop
todo.desc: Wander polished stone lanes, galleries and waterfront cafes before day-trippers arrive.

todo.name: Golden Cape forest park
todo.desc: Walk or cycle to rocky swim spots under pines close to town.

todo.name: Istrian food evening
todo.desc: Base dinner around seafood, olive oil, local wine or truffles depending on season.

todo.name: Batana heritage stop
todo.desc: Give the old fishing identity some time rather than treating Rovinj as just a photogenic backdrop.

todo.name: Island or cove boat outing
todo.desc: Use the harbour for a short-island trip or swim stop once you have seen the old town on foot.

wifi.note: Usually solid in hotels, apartments and cafes, though the old stone core and pine-fringed swim spots are the places where connectivity feels less seamless than the polished harbourfront suggests.

prac.fltNote: Rovinj has no airport. Pula is the closest gateway and easyJet currently sells London Gatwick-Pula nonstop in season; Venice Marco Polo adds more year-round airlift but at the cost of a much longer cross-border transfer. Keep destination-level nonstop flags false, because the last leg is still road or ferry logistics rather than a true fly-to city.
prac.lang: Croatian; English is common around coastal tourism
prac.visa: Schengen rules apply; UK/Irish travellers should verify 90-in-180-day limits and passport validity before travel.
alerts: July-August old-town lanes and swim spots are crowded.
Parking outside the pedestrian core needs planning.
Many boat trips and restaurants scale back in winter.

sweet: June and September are usually Rovinj's sweet spot: the sea is inviting, the harbour life is fully switched on, and the town is easier to enjoy than in the thick of July-August crowding.
